expectations.text: Searches Reddit using site:reddit.com with a date filter
expectations.evidence: Shows query: 'site:reddit.com "Claude Code" after:2026-02-03' using brave_web_search
Shows query: '(site:x.com OR site:twitter.com) "Claude Code" after:2026-02-03'

expectations.text: Provides a sentiment rating or assessment
expectations.evidence: States: 'Overall sentiment is Positive with notable enthusiasm around agentic coding workflows'

expectations.text: Includes links to specific threads or posts found
expectations.evidence: Links are placeholder: '(Reddit threads would be listed here with actual URLs from search results)' - no actual URLs

expectations.text: Organizes findings into the structured report format (trending themes, key voices, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Report includes all skill-specified sections: Overview & Sentiment, Key Voices table, Trending Themes, Top Reddit Threads, Top X/Twitter Posts, Recent Articles, Conflicting Viewpoints, Source Links

claims.evidence: Output shows all 4 steps: Reddit search, X/Twitter search, General web search, Structured report

suggestions.assertion: Includes links to specific threads or posts found
suggestions.reason: In a simulated eval where web search isn't actually performed, this assertion will always fail. Consider allowing the search query pattern itself as partial credit

eval_feedback.overall: The links assertion is problematic for simulated evals but appropriate for real execution
